open_questions_for_tony: Does Miami-Dade expose a parcel-number search? (review covers name-search only)
Is there a Party Type / Grantor-Grantee filter? (review does not mention one)
Confirm 'Miami-Date' source-docx header is a typo for Miami-Dade
notes: Miami-Dade FL — proprietary platform (custom miamidadeclerk.gov backend, NOT in the Tyler/Landmark/OnceCare/DueProcess/Clericus families). Live-portal probe on 2026-05-21 (see `probe_report` field) confirmed the official portal is an SPA shell with NO public/anonymous name-search form — every entry point funnels to `Register/Login` or the paid Developer API. Custom-scraper adapter path is NOT viable. Two paths forward, both pending a business decision: (1) commercial portal access ($1/exam or $500 for 5000) via account registration; (2) Miami-Dade Developer API (also paid + registered, likely lower per-call cost and far simpler integration than UI scraping). Adapter skeleton remains in place at `miami_dade_adapter.py` and registry wiring is live, so the surface compiles and tests pass — but search/download methods all raise NotImplementedError pointing to the probe report. If the API path is chosen, the Selenium adapter is the wrong abstraction — switch to a `MiamiDadeAPIClient` class. Original Indexing Review's adapter-implications (3-field name, two-click image, 3-day image lag, etc.) describe post-auth behaviour and remain valid as design inputs once access is unlocked.
